articles.title: Nanocoder as an ACP agent - driving it from Zed
articles.focus: A walkthrough of what changes when Nanocoder becomes an Agent Client Protocol server: how JSON-RPC replaces the terminal UI, which editor affordances (streaming, diffs, permission prompts, ask_user buttons, session modes) come for free, and how it compares to the existing WebSocket-based VS Code integration.

articles.title: Tune, auto-detected - how the new tool profiles pick themselves
articles.focus: The mechanics behind the 33-to-19 tool consolidation and the new auto tune profile that infers full, minimal, or nano from the active model's parameter count, including how it interacts with development modes, the 5-layer config hierarchy, and what local-model workflows gain from a profile that re-resolves on model switch.
